After this plan completes, proceed to Plans 3 (Cluster 03 tooling) and 4 (Cluster 04 testing). They can run in parallel.

docs.title: Execution Findings (2026-06-15)
docs.text: Plan 2 was executed end-to-end on feat/p-1.e-cluster-01-pr-fraud branch (11 commits). Findings documented inline above as Execution note callouts under each affected step. Summary of high-impact deviations:

docs.title: Plan bugs found and fixed inline
docs.text:
1. Task 1 fixture matching (regenerate-baselines.sh): original ${full}-* pattern failed because (a) G4 dispatch dict uses hyphen-form keys (shenbi-genre-config), (b) fixtures use bare skill names, (c) g4_* function suffixes use underscores. Three naming conventions had to be reconciled. Hardcoded G4_SKILLS list replaces the grep ^def g4_ loop (the loop breaks after Task 4 anyway).

  2. Task 1 baseline count: expected ≥10, actually 7. Fixture/skill naming is too loose to match all 20 G4 skills. 7 baselines (G0, G2 ×3, G4-genre_config, G6, G7) provide sufficient differential coverage.

  3. Task 2 type-checker overrides BEFORE extraction: pre-commit fails on the new shared.py unless shenbi.gates.shared is added to mypy ignore_errors, basedpyright exclude, AND ruff per-file-ignores. The plan's existing src/shenbi/*.py ruff pattern matches only top-level files, not subdirectories — needed explicit src/shenbi/gates/*.py entry.

  4. Task 3 cross-gate late imports: gates call each other (g3→g2, g5→g4, g6→g4, g7→g4+g6). Original monolith had them in one namespace. After extraction, use function-level from shenbi.gates.gN import gate_GN inside caller bodies. Add PLC0415 to ruff per-file-ignores.

  5. Task 3 temporary g4/init.py shim: cross-gate imports of gate_G4 need a stable path before Task 4 moves the checkers. Created a thin shim using importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to lazy-load validate-gate.py. Add PLW0603 to ruff per-file-ignores for the global statement.

  6. Task 4 main() bleed: scripting the extraction with "trim trailing blank lines and comments" logic accidentally pulled the entire main() function and if __name__ block into generic.py. Verify with grep -n "^def main\|^if __name__" src/shenbi/gates/g4/generic.py after writing.

  7. Task 4 chapter_drafting.py cross-module deps: uses _text_fingerprint from g5 and sys.stderr. Add import sys and convert _text_fingerprint calls to function-level late import.

  8. Task 5 reference updates missed by plan: regenerate-baselines.sh (VG constant + G4_SKILLS list), test-gates.sh (VALIDATE_GATE variable + shell quoting trap), test_gates_integrity.py (VG constant removal + new run_validate helper because run_py can't accept multi-word commands), lock-tool-hashes.sh (paths from tests/*.py to src/shenbi/*.py), cli.py basedpyright exclude.

  9. Task 6 dispatcher type-checker overrides: same pattern as gates — add shenbi.dispatcher.executor + shenbi.dispatcher.modes.* to mypy, src/shenbi/dispatcher/**/*.py to basedpyright exclude, two new ruff per-file-ignores keys.

  10. Task 8 rounds 002-007 gitignored: .gitignore:26 already excludes tests/rounds/round-NNN-YYYY-MM-DD/. The plan's git mv was a no-op. Only README for tracked archived/round-001-2026-06-11/ needed.

  11. Task 9 template dir git mv fails on empty source: used plain mv + git add -A.

  12. Task 9 baseline regen required after rename: G7's check_dir path changed from novel-output to skill-output, flipping round-001 result from PASS to SKIP. Task 1 baseline must be regenerated and recommitted.

  13. Task 10 mutants/ working dir: mutmut run creates a mutants/ directory with mutated copies of every Python file. Some files exceed 20MB. Add mutants/ to .gitignore BEFORE running.

  14. Task 10 mutmut/subprocess baseline collection failure: test_gates_integrity.py::TestGateMarkers::test_g4_fail_no_marker invokes shenbi-validate via subprocess; under mutmut's isolation the subprocess returns empty stdout, causing json.loads failure. Pre-existing test fragility, not a PR-19 regression. Actual baseline numbers deferred to follow-up.

docs.title: What worked well
docs.text:
• Strangler-fig with differential testing caught every behavior change immediately. Zero false negatives.
  • Type-checker overrides (mypy/basedpyright/ruff) following the legacy code as it moved kept pre-commit green at every commit.
  • Feature-branch-off-feature-branch pattern (Plan 2 branching off Plan 1's feat/p-1.e-pr-18-src-layout instead of main) preserved isolation without waiting for PR-18 merge.

docs.title: What did NOT work
docs.text:
• Subagent dispatch for the complex Tasks 2-5: the 4318-line validate-gate.py had to be loaded into subagent context anyway, and my own review required the same load. Direct execution was more efficient. Subagents were skipped for these tasks.
  • Mutmut baseline establishment: blocked by subprocess test fragility under mutmut isolation. Needs follow-up investigation.
  • Plan's expected baseline count of ≥10: fixture/skill naming conventions are too irregular to match all 20 G4 skills programmatically.

docs.title: P-1.E Plan 3: Tooling Configuration Invalidation (PR-24 ~ PR-27)
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

Goal: Make structlog, mypy, basedpyright, and coverage thresholds actually enforce quality. Migrate skill Python scripts into src/shenbi/skill_utils/.

Architecture: Four independent PRs. PR-24 sweeps all print() → structlog. PR-25 removes mypy/basedpyright ignore_errors + exclude overrides. PR-26 sets coverage thresholds to canonical values (80% branch / 90% line / 0.10 test density). PR-27 moves skills/*/compute_*.py to src/shenbi/skill_utils/.

Tech Stack: structlog 24+, mypy --strict, basedpyright strict, pytest-cov, pytest-density.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-p-1.e-foundation-completion/03-tooling-invalidation.md

Depends on: Plans 1 + 2 (src layout + modular code).

Blocks: Plan 5 (CI hardening needs canonical thresholds).

Estimated effort: XL (1-2 weeks).

docs.title: Task 0: Pre-flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Verify Plans 1 + 2 merged: [ -d src/shenbi ] && [ -f src/shenbi/scoring.py ] && [ -f src/shenbi/gates/cli.py ] || { echo "Plans 1+2 not merged; abort"; exit 1; } (commit messages in this repo use descriptive subjects with no PR-NN tags, so a grep on PR numbers does not work).
  • [ ] Verify Plan 2 completed: [ ! -f tests/validate-gate.py ] || { echo "Plan 2 PR-19 should have deleted tests/validate-gate.py; abort"; exit 1; }
  • [ ] Verify clean tree: git status --short empty.
  • [ ] Baseline: uv run pytest -q exits 0 (record the test count for reference; Plan 2 may have added tests since the original baseline).

docs.title: Task 1: Categorize all print() calls
docs.text:
• [ ] Step 1: Enumerate every print() in framework code
grep -rn "print(" src/shenbi/ --include='*.py' > /tmp/prints.txt
wc -l /tmp/prints.txt

Expected: ~92 lines (no test_ filter needed — src/shenbi/ contains no test files by definition after Plan 1's layout migration; tests live under tests/).

  • [ ] Step 2: Categorize each into one of:
  • DATA — emits JSON/parsed output to stdout (KEEP as print() or move to emit_json())
  • INFO — human-readable status message (log.info)
  • ERROR — error message (log.error)
  • WARN — warning (log.warning)

For each line in /tmp/prints.txt, annotate the category.

docs.title: Task 2: Add configure_logging() to each framework CLI
docs.text:
Files: src/shenbi/gates/cli.py, src/shenbi/dispatcher/cli.py, src/shenbi/scoring.py, src/shenbi/phase_runner.py, src/shenbi/summarize_round.py, src/shenbi/update_progress.py

  • [ ] Step 1: For each file's main() function, add configure_logging() as first call

  • Note: src/shenbi/gates/cli.py and src/shenbi/dispatcher/cli.py are subprocess forwarders after Plan 1; Plan 2 (PR-19/PR-20) replaces them with real implementations. Since this plan depends on Plans 1 + 2, configure_logging() applies to the real CLIs by execution time. If Plan 2 has not merged, scope this step to the files that already have real main() implementations.

Pattern (for src/shenbi/scoring.py):

def main():
    from shenbi.logging import configure_logging
    configure_logging()
    # ... rest of main

Apply same pattern to all 6 files.

  • [ ] Step 2: At top of each file, import get_logger
from shenbi.logging import get_logger
log = get_logger(__name__)
  • [ ] Step 3: Commit
git add -A
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-24): configure structlog in all framework CLIs"

docs.title: Task 3: Replace print() with log.info/error/warning
docs.text:
Files: all src/shenbi/**/*.py

For each print() line in /tmp/prints.txt (excluding DATA category):

  • [ ] Step 1: Replace section headers
print("=== G1: Input Validation ===")

→

log.info("gate_section", gate="G1", phase="input_validation")
  • [ ] Step 2: Replace pass/fail messages
print("G1 PASSED")
print(f"G1 FAILED: {msg}")

→

log.info("gate_passed", gate="G1")
log.error("gate_failed", gate="G1", reason=msg)
  • [ ] Step 3: Replace info messages
print(f"Agent ID: {agent_id}")
print(f"Dispatch mode: {mode}")

→

log.info("dispatch_start", agent_id=agent_id)
log.info("dispatch_mode", mode=mode)
  • [ ] Step 4: DATA-emitting prints stay

Any print(json.dumps(result)) that downstream consumers parse via stdout → KEEP as print() or refactor to:

def emit_json(data: dict) -> None:
    """Emit JSON to stdout for downstream parsing."""
    print(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False))

Add emit_json to a new src/shenbi/cli_utils.py (NOT to logging.py — logging is for stderr diagnostics; stdout JSON emission is a CLI output concern, and mixing them obscures the module's purpose). Create the file:

"""CLI output utilities for emitting machine-readable data to stdout.

Separate from shenbi.logging (which is for stderr diagnostics):
- logging.* → stderr, human or JSON-format logs
- cli_utils.emit_json → stdout, parsed by downstream tools (shells, other CLIs)
"""

import json
import sys
from typing import Any


def emit_json(data: Any) -> None:
    """Emit JSON to stdout for downstream parsing.

    Used by gate/scoring CLIs that produce JSON output consumed by shell
    pipelines or other tools. Distinct from structlog logging which goes
    to stderr.
    """
    sys.stdout.write(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False))
    sys.stdout.write("\n")
    sys.stdout.flush()

Then update DATA-emitting prints in gates/cli.py, scoring.py, etc.:

# Before:
print(json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False))
# After:
from shenbi.cli_utils import emit_json
emit_json(result)
  • [ ] Step 5: Verify zero print() (except emit_json)
grep -rn "print(" src/shenbi/ --include='*.py' | grep -v "emit_json"

Expected: empty. cli_utils.py::emit_json uses sys.stdout.write, not print(), so there should be zero print( matches anywhere under src/shenbi/. (The grep -v "emit_json" filter is a no-op against the sys.stdout.write implementation and can be dropped; it only matters if a future emit_json were reimplemented with print().)

  • [ ] Step 6: Test JSON logging
SHENBI_LOG_FORMAT=json uv run shenbi-validate G0 outline-example.md 2>&1 | head -5

Expected: JSON log lines on stderr.

  • [ ] Step 7: Run pytest
uv run pytest -q

Expected: pass. Some tests may need updating if they assert on print output (unlikely for unit tests).

  • [ ] Step 7b: Verify structlog integration density (spec acceptance)

The spec requires >= 50 matches:

grep -rn "structlog\|get_logger" src/shenbi/ --include='*.py' | wc -l

Expected: >= 50. With get_logger(__name__) in every framework module plus log.info/error/warning calls replacing ~92 print() calls, this should be comfortably met. If below 50, ensure every src/shenbi/**/*.py module imports get_logger and uses structured log calls (not just the 6 CLI files).

  • [ ] Step 7c: Create logging tests (spec acceptance)

The spec requires tests in tests/unit/test_logging.py covering at least 5 framework modules' logging output. Create tests/unit/test_logging.py with caplog-based tests verifying structured log fields for at least 5 modules (e.g., gates, scoring, dispatcher, phase_runner, summarize_round). Example pattern:

def test_gate_logging_has_structured_fields(caplog):
    """Verify gate logs include gate= and phase/event= fields."""
    import logging
    with caplog.at_level(logging.INFO):
        # invoke a gate that logs
        ...
    assert any("gate" in record.kwargs or "gate" in str(record.args) for record in caplog.records)

If the logging tests are deferred to Plan 4 (Cluster 04), note this explicitly in the PR description and add a tracking item.

  • [ ] Step 8: Commit
git add -A
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-24): replace 92 print() with structlog calls

All framework print() replaced by structured log calls. DATA-emitting
prints refactored to emit_json() helper. SHENBI_LOG_FORMAT=json produces
JSON lines on stderr."

docs.title: Task 4: Add type annotations module by module
docs.text:
Order: easiest first (smaller files). For each module:

  1. Add type annotations to all function signatures.
  2. Run mypy + basedpyright on that module.
  3. Fix errors.
  4. Remove from pyproject.toml overrides.

  5. Commit.

  6. [ ] Step 1: Annotate src/shenbi/scoring.py

Run: uv run mypy src/shenbi/scoring.py Expected: many errors (currently untyped).

For each function in scoring.py:

# Before:
def normalize_score(raw, rubric):
    ...

# After:
def normalize_score(raw: dict[str, int], rubric: Rubric) -> dict[str, int]:
    ...

(Define Rubric as a TypedDict or Pydantic model if needed.)

  • [ ] Step 2: Remove shenbi.scoring from mypy overrides

Plan 1 Task 9.5 already converted the old tests.* module list to shenbi.* equivalents. Do NOT search for the pre-Plan-1 tests.* block; it no longer exists. The block now reads (the actual post-Plan-1 state):

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "tests.validate-gate",
    "shenbi.scoring",            # ← REMOVE this line
    "shenbi.phase_runner",
    "shenbi.summarize_round",
    "shenbi.update_progress",
    "shenbi.gates",
    "shenbi.gates.cli",
    "shenbi.dispatcher",
    "shenbi.dispatcher.cli",
    "tests.unit.test_gates_integrity",
]
ignore_errors = true

Remove only the shenbi.scoring line (leave the rest for their own modules). Each remaining module is removed one-by-one in Step 6 as its annotations land.

  • [ ] Step 3: Update basedpyright exclude

In pyproject.toml, find [tool.basedpyright] exclude list. Remove "src/shenbi/scoring.py" (Plan 1 Task 9.5 Step 3 already converted this from the old tests/scoring.py path). Do not search for tests/scoring.py; it no longer exists in the exclude list.

  • [ ] Step 4: Verify scoring.py passes
uv run mypy src/shenbi/scoring.py
uv run basedpyright src/shenbi/scoring.py

Both must exit 0.

  • [ ] Step 5: Commit
git add -A
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-25): annotate scoring.py + remove from type-checker overrides"
  • [ ] Step 6: Repeat for remaining modules

Repeat Steps 1-5 for each: - src/shenbi/phase_runner.py - src/shenbi/summarize_round.py - src/shenbi/update_progress.py - src/shenbi/gates/g0.py through g7.py - src/shenbi/gates/shared.py - src/shenbi/gates/g4/generic.py - src/shenbi/gates/g4/<skill>.py (23 modules: 3 generic + 20 per-skill) - src/shenbi/dispatcher/executor.py - src/shenbi/dispatcher/modes/*.py - tests/unit/test_gates_integrity.py

Also remove tests.validate-gate from the ignore_errors module list (Plan 2 PR-19 Step 4 deleted tests/validate-gate.py, so this entry is now dead; Plan 2 does not touch the mypy overrides).

One commit per module. The normalize_score example in Step 1 is illustrative only -- each module has distinct function signatures and internal logic. Read each module's actual signatures before annotating.

Once all 23 g4 modules are annotated, also remove the separate g4 override block that Plan 1 Task 9.5 added:

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "shenbi.gates.g4.*"
disallow_untyped_defs = false

This graduated suppression is a residual escape hatch (the spec's Root Cause 3 calls graduated suppressions dishonest if left in place). It must be removed in the final g4 commit; it is NOT covered by the ignore_errors block above.

  • [ ] Step 7: Verify ZERO overrides remain
grep -nE "ignore_errors|disallow_untyped_defs|exclude.*shenbi" pyproject.toml

Expected: empty output (no ignore_errors, no disallow_untyped_defs, no shenbi paths in basedpyright exclude; only __pycache__, archived, round-[0-9] patterns should remain in the exclude list). This grep must catch the shenbi.gates.g4.* disallow_untyped_defs = false block; if it still appears, remove it before committing.

  • [ ] Step 8: Final type check
uv run mypy src/shenbi/
uv run basedpyright

Both must exit 0.

  • [ ] Step 9: Commit (final PR-25)
git add -A
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-25): complete type annotations + zero ignore_errors overrides

All framework modules now pass mypy --strict and basedpyright --strict.
No escape hatches remain."

docs.title: Task 5: Set canonical thresholds
docs.text:
Files: pyproject.toml, tests/unit/test_coverage_thresholds.py

  • [ ] Step 1: Update pyproject.toml fail_under

The --cov=shenbi addopts entry is already set by Plan 1 Step 2b; no change needed there. Verify coverage now measures framework code (not tests):

uv run pytest -q --cov=shenbi --cov-report=term | head -20

Expected: the report lists src/shenbi/**/*.py modules (scoring, gates, etc.), NOT tests/**.

Then bump the line-coverage threshold:

Find: fail_under = 8 Replace: fail_under = 90

  • [ ] Step 2: Update BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT in tests/unit/test_coverage_thresholds.py

Find: BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT = 0.5 Replace: BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT = 80

  • [ ] Step 3: Create tests/unit/test_test_density.py

Note: this version counts ast.AsyncFunctionDef in addition to ast.FunctionDef. The spec's reference implementation counts only ast.FunctionDef, but this project uses asyncio_mode = "auto" so async test functions are valid. This is a deliberate, documented improvement over the spec.

"""Enforce test density floor per README Threshold Justification.

Metric: test_function_count / framework_loc
Target: >= 0.10 (1 test function per 10 LOC of framework code).
"""

import ast
from pathlib import Path

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
FRAMEWORK_DIR = REPO_ROOT / "src" / "shenbi"
TEST_DIRS = [REPO_ROOT / "tests" / sub for sub in ("unit", "integration", "property", "benchmark")]


def count_framework_loc() -> int:
    """Count non-blank, non-comment Python LOC in src/shenbi/."""
    total = 0
    for py_file in FRAMEWORK_DIR.rglob("*.py"):
        for line in py_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
            stripped = line.strip()
            if stripped and not stripped.startswith("#"):
                total += 1
    return total


def count_test_functions() -> int:
    """Count `def test_*` (and `async def test_*`) functions across all test directories."""
    total = 0
    for test_dir in TEST_DIRS:
        if not test_dir.exists():
            continue
        for py_file in test_dir.rglob("*.py"):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(py_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            except SyntaxError:
                continue
            for node in ast.walk(tree):
                if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)) and node.name.startswith("test_"):
                    total += 1
    return total


def test_density_meets_minimum():
    framework_loc = count_framework_loc()
    test_count = count_test_functions()
    density = test_count / framework_loc if framework_loc else 0
    assert density >= 0.10, (
        f"Test density {density:.4f} below 0.10 floor "
        f"({test_count} tests / {framework_loc} framework LOC). "
        f"Add ~{int(0.10 * framework_loc - test_count)} more tests."
    )
  • [ ] Step 4: Run density test (expect fail)
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_test_density.py -v

Expected: FAIL with density < 0.10 (Cluster 04 will add tests to meet this).

  • [ ] Step 5: Commit threshold config (will fail CI until Cluster 04 completes)
git add pyproject.toml tests/unit/test_test_density.py
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-26): set coverage thresholds (80% branch, 90% line, 0.10 density)

NOTE: Density test will fail until Cluster 04 PR-28~32 deliver ~600 tests.
This is intentional — thresholds enforce the canonical values per
README Threshold Justification."
  • [ ] Step 5b: Update README coverage badge (spec acceptance)

The spec PR-26 acceptance requires "Coverage badge in README points to actual coverage." Update the README coverage badge URL to point to the coverage report or CI coverage job. If no badge exists yet, add one:

[![Coverage](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=<coverage-endpoint>)](<coverage-report-url>)

The exact badge URL depends on the CI provider configured in Plan 5; use a placeholder pointing to the CI coverage artifact and update it when Plan 5 lands.

  • [ ] Step 6: Merge ordering — must land AFTER Plan 4 (Cluster 04)

Critical sequencing rule: PR-26 must merge only after Plan 4 PR-28 ~ PR-32 deliver ~600 tests. If PR-26 merges before Plan 4, CI fails on main until Plan 4 catches up — a red main blocks all other PRs.

Two ways to enforce this:

  1. Preferred — land PR-26 last in P-1.E sequence: hold the PR open until Plan 4 merges, then rebase + merge. PR-26's diff is tiny (one fail_under change + one new test file), so rebase cost is near zero.

  2. Alternative — temporary xfail: mark test_density_meets_minimum() with @pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True, reason="Plan 4 PR-28~32 will deliver remaining tests"). This keeps the density test green. Caveat: xfail does NOT cover the fail_under = 90 enforcement. pytest-cov exits non-zero at session teardown and that exit code cannot be xfail-ed. So the xfail option alone leaves main red on the coverage gate. To stay fully green you must ALSO temporarily lower fail_under (or pass --no-cov-fail-under) and revert both in the same Plan 4 PR. Remove the xfail in the same Plan 4 PR that delivers the 600th test (otherwise strict xfail flips to XPASS = failure). Given this caveat, option 1 (land last) is the only zero-config safe path.

If neither option is followed and main goes red, the fix is to revert PR-26 entirely until Plan 4 catches up — do not lower the threshold as a workaround.

docs.title: Task 6: Move skill Python scripts
docs.text:
Files: - Move: skills/shenbi-chapter-pattern/compute_pattern.py → src/shenbi/skill_utils/chapter_pattern/compute_pattern.py - Move: skills/shenbi-style-learning/compute_stats.py → src/shenbi/skill_utils/style_learning/compute_stats.py - Modify: skills/shenbi-chapter-pattern/SKILL.md, skills/shenbi-style-learning/SKILL.md - Modify: pyproject.toml ruff config

  • [ ] Step 1: Create target directories
mkdir -p src/shenbi/skill_utils/chapter_pattern src/shenbi/skill_utils/style_learning
touch src/shenbi/skill_utils/__init__.py
touch src/shenbi/skill_utils/chapter_pattern/__init__.py
touch src/shenbi/skill_utils/style_learning/__init__.py
  • [ ] Step 2: Move files
git mv skills/shenbi-chapter-pattern/compute_pattern.py src/shenbi/skill_utils/chapter_pattern/compute_pattern.py
git mv skills/shenbi-style-learning/compute_stats.py src/shenbi/skill_utils/style_learning/compute_stats.py

After the move, both files still have if __name__ == "__main__": main() blocks (compute_pattern.py:175, compute_stats.py:395). These are now redundant under -m invocation but harmless. Remove them in Step 5 (when adding type annotations) to avoid confusion.

  • [ ] Step 3: Add __main__.py for standalone execution

src/shenbi/skill_utils/chapter_pattern/__main__.py:

"""Standalone runner: python -m shenbi.skill_utils.chapter_pattern"""
from shenbi.skill_utils.chapter_pattern.compute_pattern import main
import sys
sys.exit(main())

Same for style_learning/__main__.py.

Note: both compute_pattern.py:main() and compute_stats.py:main() currently return None (they call print() for output and sys.exit(1) on error paths). sys.exit(None) exits 0, so this works correctly. No change to the main() functions is required for this step.

  • [ ] Step 4: Update ruff config — remove skills exclusion

In pyproject.toml, find [tool.ruff]:

extend-exclude = [
    "tests/rounds/archived",
    "tests/rounds/round-[0-9][0-9][0-9]-*",
    "skills",
]

Replace skills exclusion with markdown-only pattern:

extend-exclude = [
    "tests/rounds/archived",
    "tests/rounds/round-[0-9][0-9][0-9]-*",
]

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"skills/**/*.md" = ["*"]  # Skill markdown files aren't Python
  • [ ] Step 5: Add type annotations to compute_pattern.py and compute_stats.py

Apply same pattern as Task 4 (PR-25). Add annotations, run mypy, fix errors, commit.

  • [ ] Step 6: Add unit tests for both scripts

Per spec PR-27 acceptance, both scripts need unit tests with >= 90% line AND >= 80% branch coverage (the spec explicitly requires this; it is not deferred to Plan 4). Create tests/unit/skill_utils/test_chapter_pattern.py and tests/unit/skill_utils/test_style_learning.py (or a single tests/unit/test_skill_utils.py) covering:

  • compute_pattern.py: pattern classification aggregation, entropy/distribution math, JSON I/O edge cases.
  • compute_stats.py: statistical computations, multi-file aggregation, --output JSON serialization.

Run after writing:

uv run pytest tests/unit/ -k "chapter_pattern or style_learning or skill_utils" -v \
  --cov=shenbi.skill_utils --cov-branch --cov-report=term-missing

Expected: both modules >= 90% line / >= 80% branch.

Density requirement: moving 568 LOC into src/shenbi/skill_utils/ raises the density denominator by 568. At the 0.10 density floor, this requires ~57 additional test functions (beyond the coverage-minimum tests above). Ensure the test count for these two modules is at least 57 test functions total (e.g., ~25 for compute_pattern.py and ~32 for compute_stats.py) to satisfy the PR-26 density gate. This is separate from and in addition to the >= 90% line / >= 80% branch coverage requirement.

  • [ ] Step 7: Update skill SKILL.md files

The actual references differ per skill; update each literally.

skills/shenbi-chapter-pattern/SKILL.md references compute_pattern.py in three places. Lines 15-16 are DOT flowchart node labels (conceptual references, not commands); line 21 is bold Chinese prose mentioning compute_pattern.py as a concept. These prose/flowchart mentions should keep the short name compute_pattern.py (replacing them with the full module invocation would make DOT labels and prose unreadable). There is no explicit command invocation in this SKILL.md to update; the flowchart already says "Run compute_pattern.py" which remains accurate. If you want to point users to the new invocation, add a single line after the flowchart:

Run: `python -m shenbi.skill_utils.chapter_pattern <args>`

skills/shenbi-style-learning/SKILL.md line 23 has the literal command python3 skills/shenbi-style-learning/compute_stats.py <chapter_files> --output /tmp/style-stats.json. This is an explicit command invocation and must be updated. Replace with:

Run: `python -m shenbi.skill_utils.style_learning <chapter_files> --output /tmp/style-stats.json`

Lines 10, 16, 17, 33 are prose/flowchart references to compute_stats.py. These are conceptual mentions (not commands). Update them to say shenbi.skill_utils.style_learning.compute_stats for accuracy, but keep them concise -- do not insert full python -m ... command strings into prose.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• PR-26 blocks on Plan 4 (Cluster 04 testing) — density test won't pass until ~600 tests exist.
  • PR-27 is independent — can land anytime after Plan 1. Note: PR-27 moves 568 LOC into src/shenbi/skill_utils/, raising the density denominator by 568 (~57 more tests needed to hold 0.10). Cross-plan risk: Plan 4's density math uses 5921 framework LOC (611 tests / 5921 = 0.103). After PR-27 merges, the denominator becomes ~6489, making 611/6489 = 0.094 < 0.10. Plan 4 never mentions skill_utils in its test budget. Resolution: PR-27 Step 6 must deliver at least 57 additional tests (beyond the >= 90% line / >= 80% branch coverage minimum) to offset the 568 LOC increase. Alternatively, PR-27 must merge after Plan 4 completes and Plan 4's test count is rechecked against the post-PR-27 LOC. Either way, the density gate (PR-26) will fail if this is not addressed.

docs.title: P-1.E Plan 4: Testing Foundation (PR-28 ~ PR-34)
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans.

Goal: Deliver ~600 tests across framework modules to meet 0.10 test density target, 80% branch coverage, 90% line coverage. Replace subprocess-based tests with direct-import unit tests.

Architecture: Seven PRs, one per test category. PR-28~31 add tests for individual modules (scoring, phase_runner, update_progress, summarize_round). PR-32 adds gates tests replacing subprocess stubs. PR-33 commits Hypothesis counterexamples. PR-34 changes pytest config to explicit --ignore.

Tech Stack: pytest 8+, pytest-cov, pytest-xdist, hypothesis 6.97+, pytest-randomly.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-p-1.e-foundation-completion/04-testing-foundation.md

Depends on: Plans 1 + 2 (src layout + modular code).

Blocks: PR-26 density test passes only after this plan delivers test count.

Estimated effort: XL (2-3 weeks; mostly test-writing).

  • [ ] Step 1: Write test file skeleton
"""Unit tests for shenbi.scoring.

Coverage target: 90% line, 80% branch.
"""

import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from shenbi.scoring import (
    main,
    load_rubric,
    load_applicability,
    filter_dimensions_by_test_type,
    validate_scores,
    compute_score,
    classify,
    check_gate_markers,
)


@pytest.fixture
def sample_rubric(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:
    """Create a minimal rubric.md matching load_rubric's real table format.

    load_rubric parses a markdown table whose header row starts with '| #' and
    returns a tuple (dimensions, kill_switches). Each dimension dict uses keys
    num/name/weight with weights parsed as integer percentages that should sum
    to 100. See src/shenbi/scoring.py. Do NOT use YAML frontmatter here:
    load_rubric ignores it and would return an empty dimensions list.
    """
    rubric = tmp_path / "rubric.md"
    rubric.write_text(
        "# Sample rubric\n\n"
        "| # | name | weight |\n"
        "|---|------|--------|\n"
        "| 1 | creativity | 60 |\n"
        "| 2 | consistency | 40 |\n\n"
        "## Kill Switches\n\n"
        "- total score = 0 on plagiarism\n"
    )
    return rubric


@pytest.fixture
def sample_scores(tmp_path: Path) -> Path:
    """Create a scores.json fixture (string keys on disk; main() casts to int)."""
    scores = tmp_path / "scores.json"
    # main() loads this with {int(k): v ...}. For direct compute_score unit
    # tests, build an int-keyed dict instead, e.g. {1: 90, 2: 80}.
    scores.write_text(json.dumps({"1": 90, "2": 80}))
    return scores
  • [ ] Step 2: Write rubric loading tests
class TestLoadRubric:
    def test_loads_valid_rubric(self, sample_rubric):
        # load_rubric returns a tuple (dimensions, kill_switches); dims use
        # num/name/weight. This doubles as a fail-fast shape smoke test.
        dimensions, kill_switches = load_rubric(sample_rubric)
        assert isinstance(dimensions, list)
        assert len(dimensions) == 2
        assert dimensions[0]["num"] == 1
        assert dimensions[0]["name"] == "creativity"
        assert dimensions[0]["weight"] == 60
        assert sum(d["weight"] for d in dimensions) == 100
        assert isinstance(kill_switches, list)

    def test_handles_missing_dimensions(self, tmp_path):
        rubric = tmp_path / "empty.md"
        rubric.write_text("---\n---\n# empty")
        # No markdown table -> load_rubric returns ([], []).
        dimensions, kill_switches = load_rubric(rubric)
        assert dimensions == []

    # ... 8 more rubric loading tests

  • [ ] Step 3: Write score computation tests (~10 tests covering compute_score, classify, weight handling, kill_switch behavior)

  • [ ] Step 4: Write test-type filtering tests (~5 tests for filter_dimensions_by_test_type covering generative/bug-hunt/clean)

  • [ ] Step 5: Write gate marker enforcement tests (~8 tests for check_gate_markers)

  • [ ] Step 6: Write REJECT behavior tests (~5 tests covering the REJECT/ACCEPT gating in validate_scores)

  • [ ] Step 7: Write subagent flag tests (~5 tests covering main()'s --subagent argument handling)

  • [ ] Step 8: Write main() integration tests (~10 tests)

class TestMain:
    def test_main_returns_zero_on_pass(self, sample_rubric, sample_scores, tmp_path, monkeypatch):
        round_dir = tmp_path / "round"
        round_dir.mkdir()
        # ... set up markers
        # main() reads sys.argv; patch it. Do NOT refactor main()'s signature
        # in this PR -- that is a source change outside a test PR's scope and
        # needs its own commit + coverage. Keep all main() tests on monkeypatch.
        monkeypatch.setattr("sys.argv", [
            "shenbi-score", str(sample_rubric), str(sample_scores),
            "--test-type", "generative", "--round-dir", str(round_dir),
        ])
        rc = main()
        assert rc == 0
  • [ ] Step 9: Write property-based tests
# tests/unit/test_scoring_property.py
"""Property-based tests for shenbi.scoring using Hypothesis."""

from hypothesis import given, strategies as st

from shenbi.scoring import classify, compute_score


@given(st.integers(min_value=0, max_value=100))
def test_classify_returns_known_label(score):
    label = classify(score)
    # Real labels from classify() in src/shenbi/scoring.py:
    #   >= 90 -> "PASS (excellent)", >= 75 -> "PASS (acceptable)",
    #   >= 60 -> "CONDITIONAL", else -> "FAIL".
    assert label in {"PASS (excellent)", "PASS (acceptable)", "CONDITIONAL", "FAIL"}


@given(
    st.dictionaries(
        keys=st.integers(min_value=1, max_value=10),
        values=st.integers(min_value=0, max_value=100),
        min_size=1,
    )
)
def test_compute_score_in_range(scores):
    # Build dimensions whose num keys cover `scores`, weights summing to 100,
    # then assert 0 <= compute_score(dimensions, scores) <= 100. Use the
    # sample_rubric markdown-table format (num/name/weight); generate dimensions
    # to match the strategy's key range (equal weights, remainder on last).
    ...

Note: test_compute_score_in_range must be fully implemented (strategy + assertion) before commit -- the ... above is scaffolding. A committed no-op property test asserts nothing and inflates the count without value.

uv run pytest tests/unit/test_scoring.py tests/unit/test_scoring_property.py -v --cov=shenbi.scoring --cov-report=term-missing

Expected: ≥ 60 tests pass, coverage ≥ 90% line, ≥ 80% branch on shenbi.scoring.

docs.title: PR-32: Direct-Import Tests for Gates (Replace Subprocess Tests)
Task 5: Move existing tests + write new direct-import tests
docs.text:
Files: - Move: tests/unit/test_gates_integrity.py → tests/integration/test_gate_cli.py (rename + reclassify) - Create: tests/unit/gates/test_shared.py - Create: tests/unit/gates/test_g0.py ... test_g7.py - Create: tests/unit/gates/g4/test_generic.py - Create: tests/unit/gates/g4/test_<skill>.py for each of 20 skill checkers - Create: tests/unit/gates/__init__.py - Create: tests/unit/gates/g4/__init__.py

  • [ ] Step 1: Create directory structure
mkdir -p tests/unit/gates/g4
touch tests/unit/gates/__init__.py tests/unit/gates/g4/__init__.py
  • [ ] Step 2: Move existing subprocess tests to integration/
git mv tests/unit/test_gates_integrity.py tests/integration/test_gate_cli.py

After the move, migrate subprocess calls in tests/integration/test_gate_cli.py from the legacy script path to the shenbi-validate entry point. Note: PR-19 (Plan 2) uses strangler-fig, so tests/validate-gate.py is NOT deleted -- it is reduced to a thin CLI dispatcher that imports from src/shenbi/gates/. The old subprocess invocations still work; switch them to shenbi-validate to exercise the real modular CLI:

Exact substitution: the moved file defines a path constant VG (line 11) and a _run_vg helper (line 18) that calls subprocess.run([os.environ.get("PYTHON", "python3"), str(VG)] + list(args), ...). There is also a standalone subprocess.run at line 183 with the same [os.environ.get("PYTHON", "python3"), str(script)] shape. Rewrite both call sites:

  • _run_vg helper: replace [os.environ.get("PYTHON", "python3"), str(VG)] with ["shenbi-validate"] (or ["uv", "run", "shenbi-validate"] when not inside an activated env). The PYTHON env lookup and the VG path constant become dead code -- remove them once no call site references them.
  • Standalone subprocess.run (line ~183): same substitution for its argv.
# Before (legacy script path; still works post-PR-19 but bypasses the modular CLI):
result = subprocess.run(
    [os.environ.get("PYTHON", "python3"), str(VG), "G0", str(seed)],
    capture_output=True, text=True,
)

# After:
result = subprocess.run(
    ["uv", "run", "shenbi-validate", "G0", str(seed)],
    capture_output=True, text=True,
)

Or, if tests run inside an already-activated uv environment, drop the uv run prefix and call shenbi-validate directly (it's on PATH after uv sync). If the entry point is not registered (Plan 1 PR-18 not merged), fall back to ["uv", "run", "python", "-m", "shenbi.gates.cli", ...] -- but the pre-flight smoke check should have caught that case first.

Verify no legacy-path references remain after the rewrite:

grep -nE "str\(VG\)|os\.environ\.get\(\"PYTHON\"|validate-gate\.py" tests/integration/test_gate_cli.py
Expected: no matches. Any remaining match is a call site that still bypasses the modular CLI; rewrite it before commit.

Verify with: uv run pytest tests/integration/test_gate_cli.py -v — all subprocess tests must pass.

  • [ ] Step 3: Write tests/unit/gates/test_shared.py (~15 tests)
"""Unit tests for shenbi.gates.shared."""

import json
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from shenbi.gates.shared import (
    jload, yload, word_count_md, passed, fail,
    write_gate_marker, _normalize_file_paths, count_transition_words,
)


class TestJload:
    def test_loads_valid_json(self, tmp_path):
        f = tmp_path / "test.json"
        f.write_text('{"a": 1}')
        assert jload(f) == {"a": 1}

    def test_raises_on_invalid_json(self, tmp_path):
        f = tmp_path / "bad.json"
        f.write_text("not json")
        with pytest.raises(json.JSONDecodeError):
            jload(f)

    # ... 5 more


class TestYload:
    def test_loads_yaml_with_frontmatter(self, tmp_path):
        f = tmp_path / "test.md"
        f.write_text("---\nkey: value\n---\ncontent")
        assert yload(f) == {"key": "value"}

    # ... 5 more


class TestWordCountMd:
    def test_counts_cjk_excluding_code_blocks(self, tmp_path):
        # word_count_md counts CJK characters only (re.findall(r"[​一​-​鿿​]", c));
        # ASCII text returns 0.
        f = tmp_path / "test.md"
        f.write_text("​世界​你好​\n\n```python\nprint('ignored')\n```\n\n​更​多​文字​")
        assert word_count_md(f) == 8  # 8 CJK chars; code block excluded

    def test_ascii_only_returns_zero(self, tmp_path):
        f = tmp_path / "test.md"
        f.write_text("hello world\n\nmore text")
        assert word_count_md(f) == 0


class TestPassedFailFormat:
    def test_passed_returns_valid_json(self):
        result = passed("G0", [{"check": "exists", "status": "pass"}])
        parsed = json.loads(result)
        assert parsed["status"] == "PASS"
        assert parsed["gate"] == "G0"

    def test_fail_returns_must_fix_list(self):
        result = fail("G0", [], "scoring", ["fix the seed"])
        parsed = json.loads(result)
        assert parsed["status"] == "FAIL"
        assert "fix the seed" in parsed["must_fix"]
  • [ ] Step 4: Write tests/unit/gates/test_g0.py (~10 tests)

Before writing tests: read the actual G0 implementation in src/shenbi/gates/g0.py (PR-19 extracted gate_G0 here from the former tests/validate-gate.py lines 277-814; this plan runs after PR-19, so read g0.py, not the legacy file). Note the real validation criteria (seed format requirements, structure expectations, file size limits, etc.). Tests must reflect actual G0 behavior — generic "PASS with valid seed" tests are insufficient if G0 requires specific structural elements (e.g., heading levels, frontmatter, minimum length).

"""Unit tests for G0 gate."""

import pytest
from pathlib import Path
from shenbi.gates.g0 import gate_G0


class TestGateG0:
    def test_passes_with_valid_seed(self, tmp_path):
        """Seed file meeting G0's actual criteria (see g0.py) returns PASS."""
        seed = tmp_path / "outline.md"
        # G0 is not a generic "file exists" check. Verified against the legacy
        # tests/validate-gate.py gate_G0 (the source PR-19 extracts): G0.2
        # requires a `​目标​字数​：<N>` line (regex `​目标​字数​[：:]\s*(\d+)`), and
        # later checks reference chapter-word floor / novel-output dir. The
        # minimal "# My Novel" string FAILS G0.2. For a guaranteed-PASS positive
        # fixture, copy the real seed that G0 is known to pass:
        import shutil
        shutil.copy(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2] / "outline-example.md", seed)
        # (adjust the parents[] depth to the repo root from this test's location)
        result = gate_G0(seed_file=str(seed))
        assert '"status": "PASS"' in result

    def test_fails_without_seed(self, tmp_path):
        """Missing seed file returns FAIL."""
        result = gate_G0(seed_file=str(tmp_path / "nonexistent.md"))
        assert '"status": "FAIL"' in result

    # ... 8 more tests covering actual G0 rules (criterion by criterion)

Test naming convention: each test name should describe the business rule being verified (e.g., test_rejects_seed_without_setup_heading, test_rejects_seed_under_500_words), not just the function call. Tests that don't encode business value (per global rule ​八​ in CLAUDE.md) should be rewritten before commit.

  • [ ] Step 5: Write tests/unit/gates/test_g1.py, test_g2.py, ..., test_g7.py

Same pattern, ~10 tests per gate. Each test verifies a specific G{n} business rule, not just "function returns PASS/FAIL". Read the gate implementation in src/shenbi/gates/g{n}.py before writing tests.

  • [ ] Step 6: Write tests/unit/gates/g4/test_generic.py (~15 tests)

Tests for g4_generic_generative, g4_generic_bughunt, g4_generic_clean.

For each of 20 skills with a g4_<skill> function, create tests/unit/gates/g4/test_<skill>.py:

from shenbi.gates.g4.worldbuilding import g4_worldbuilding


class TestG4Worldbuilding:

        # Set up minimal valid worldbuilding output
        ...
        result = g4_worldbuilding([str(world_bible)], rd=tmp_path)
        assert '"status": "PASS"' in result

    # ... 10+ tests per skill

Verified: grep -c "^def g4_" tests/validate-gate.py = 23 (20 per-skill + 3 generic), not 28. Target 12 tests per skill (240 total for 20 skills) to clear the 0.10 density floor. Do not reduce below 12 per skill -- at 8/skill the budget only reaches ~531 tests (density 0.090 < 0.10), failing PR-26's density gate.

uv run pytest tests/unit/gates/ -v --cov=shenbi.gates --cov-report=term-missing
uv run pytest --collect-only -q | tail -1

docs.title: Task 6: Configure .hypothesis/examples/ to commit
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: .hypothesis/.gitignore - Create: .hypothesis/examples/.gitkeep (tracks the dir even with no counterexamples yet)

  • [ ] Step 1: Replace .hypothesis/.gitignore
mkdir -p .hypothesis/examples
cat > .hypothesis/.gitignore <<'EOF'
# Hypothesis caches are machine-specific
cache/

# Examples ARE committed — they capture discovered counterexamples
# and replay them on every run, ensuring regression detection.
!examples/
!examples/**
EOF
touch .hypothesis/examples/.gitkeep
  • [ ] Step 2: Run tests with property-based tests to populate examples
uv run pytest tests/unit/test_scoring_property.py tests/unit/test_phase_runner_property.py -v

This generates .hypothesis/examples/ with any counterexamples found.

  • [ ] Step 3: Verify examples directory populated
ls -la .hypothesis/examples/
git status --short .hypothesis/examples/

Expected: .gitkeep is present and tracked. Counterexample binaries appear only if property tests found bugs (additive, not a gating criterion -- the dir is tracked regardless).

Spec divergence (documented): the spec PR-33 Acceptance requires ".hypothesis/examples/ exists with at least 1 committed counterexample" and "CI verifies that committed examples still reproduce." This plan relaxes that to a conditional: the .gitignore reconfiguration and dir tracking land unconditionally; an actual counterexample is committed only if the PR-28/PR-29 property tests genuinely surface a failing case during this run. Forcing a counterexample would require manufacturing a bug that does not exist. If property tests find a real counterexample, commit it and the spec acceptance is met in full. If none is found, this plan satisfies the persistence mechanism (tracked dir + replayable examples) but defers the CI-replay assertion to Plan 5 (CI supply chain), which owns the CI job that replays committed examples. Record which case applied in the PR description so the spec gap is explicit, not silent.

  • [ ] Step 4: Commit
git add .hypothesis/.gitignore .hypothesis/examples/
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-33): persist Hypothesis counterexamples in git

.hypothesis/examples/ tracked via .gitkeep. Discovered counterexamples
replay on every run, catching regressions even if random seed differs.
Lands unconditionally; counterexamples are additive, not gating."

If a real counterexample was produced in Step 2, note it in the commit body (Found counterexample: <test>::<case>). The spec's ">= 1 committed counterexample" acceptance is met only when one is present; otherwise this PR delivers the mechanism and defers replay assertion to Plan 5.

docs.title: Task 7: Change testpaths to explicit norecursedirs
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: pyproject.toml

  • [ ] Step 1: Update pytest config

Find:

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests/unit", "tests/integration", "tests/property", "tests/benchmark"]

Replace with:

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
norecursedirs = [
    "tests/rounds",
    "tests/fixtures",
    "tests/baselines",
    "tests/coverage",
    "tests/tiers",
    "tests/dispatcher",
    "tests/benchmarks",
    "tests/pressure-tests",
    "tests/skill-behavior",
    "tests/skill-triggering",
]
# This list deliberately exceeds the spec's 4 entries (rounds, fixtures,
# baselines, coverage): tests/tiers, tests/dispatcher, tests/benchmarks,
# tests/pressure-tests, tests/skill-behavior, tests/skill-triggering hold
# non-test Python/data (round configs, dispatch wrappers, etc.) that would be
# swept into collection under testpaths=["tests"]. Keep the superset; do not
# trim back to 4. Note the singular/plural footgun: tests/benchmark (singular,
# collected) vs tests/benchmarks (plural, excluded) are different dirs.
# Spec Open Question #2: real skill dispatches live in tests/e2e/ behind
# @pytest.mark.e2e, skipped in CI (pytest -m "not e2e"). Plan 5 wires the
# marker + CI skip; create tests/e2e/ when the first e2e test lands.
  • [ ] Step 1b: Enforce the advertised 90% line / 80% branch thresholds

Plan 1 (PR-18) already moved [tool.coverage.run].source to ["src/shenbi"] and pytest addopts --cov from tests to shenbi. But the gate thresholds were never raised: fail_under is still 8 and tests/unit/test_coverage_thresholds.py still has BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT = 0.5. This plan delivers the tests that reach 90/80, so enforce them here.

Ownership note (resolves double-edit with Plan 3 PR-26): Plan 3 PR-26 also edits fail_under and BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT (its Step 1 + the commit at 03-tooling.md:447). This step (PR-34 Step 1b) is the authoritative owner of the threshold raise because it is the plan that delivers the tests meeting 90/80. PR-26 must NOT also set these two values; PR-26 keeps only the density test file and its sequencing rule. Whichever PR lands second would otherwise no-op or conflict on the same two lines (pyproject.toml fail_under, test_coverage_thresholds.py BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT). If PR-26 has already landed its threshold edits, treat this step as a no-op verification (confirm the values are already 90/80); otherwise apply them here.

In pyproject.toml [tool.coverage.report]: change fail_under = 8 to fail_under = 90 (update the staging comment -- this cluster reaches the full 90/80 target, superseding the earlier "30% after P-1.E" staging). In tests/unit/test_coverage_thresholds.py: change BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT = 0.5 to BRANCH_THRESHOLD_PCT = 80.

uv run pytest -q --cov-branch --cov-report=term

Expected: line >= 90%, branch >= 80%, exit 0. If a module falls short, add tests for it -- do not lower the threshold (that is a cost compromise).

  • [ ] Step 2: Verify pytest collects expected tests (no count regression)

Capture the pre-switch count first (before editing testpaths), then after:

uv run pytest --collect-only -q | tail -3

Expected: ~654 tests collected (43 existing + 611 new = 654), and the count must equal the pre-switch count. (The >= 600 floor is the spec target; ~654 is the concrete grand total this plan delivers.) A change means a norecursedirs entry is missing (e.g. tests/dispatcher/ would otherwise import cli.py at collection time).

  • [ ] Step 3: Verify exclusions work
uv run pytest --collect-only tests/rounds/ -q

Expected: no tests ran (rounds excluded).

  • [ ] Step 4: Commit
git add pyproject.toml
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-34): switch pytest testpaths to norecursedirs

Old: testpaths=['tests/unit', 'tests/integration', ...] (implicit exclusion)
New: testpaths=['tests'] + explicit norecursedirs for rounds, fixtures,
baselines, coverage. New test dirs auto-collect."

docs.title: Task 8: Final verification — density + coverage
docs.text:
• [ ] Step 1: Test density meets the 0.10 floor

The dedicated density test (tests/unit/test_test_density.py) is delivered by downstream Plan 3 PR-26, so do not invoke it here. Compute density directly:

tests=$(uv run pytest --collect-only -q 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}')
loc=$(find src/shenbi -name '*.py' | xargs wc -l | tail -1 | awk '{print $1}')
python3 -c "d=$tests/$loc; print(f'density={d:.4f}'); assert d >= 0.10, 'below 0.10'"

Expected: density >= 0.10. Use awk '{print $1}' to take only the test-count token from pytest's summary line (e.g. 611 tests collected in 0.98s); grep -oE '[0-9]+' would capture 611, 0, and 98 and silently parse the shell as density=611.0000, exiting 0 on a false positive.

Denominator reconciliation with Plan 3 PR-27: PR-27 moves ~568 LOC of skills/*/compute_*.py (chapter_pattern 176 + style_learning 392) into src/shenbi/skill_utils/ -- inside this find src/shenbi scope. So the denominator depends on PR-27's landing state:

  • PR-27 NOT landed: ~5921 LOC -> needs >= 593 tests.
  • PR-27 landed: ~6489 LOC -> needs >= 649 tests.

This plan's 611-test budget only clears 0.10 if PR-27 has NOT landed yet (611/5921 = 0.103; but 611/6489 = 0.094 < 0.10). PR-27 Step 6 (Plan 3) adds its own skill_utils unit tests, which raise the numerator when PR-27 lands. Ordering rule: $loc is recomputed against the live tree here (it shells out to find), so the command self-corrects to whichever denominator exists. If density < 0.10 prints, add tests (raise the per-skill G4 floor or add property tests) until the live denominator clears 0.10 -- do not lower the floor. PR-26 (Plan 3, the density gate) must merge only after this recomputation passes.

  • [ ] Step 2: Coverage thresholds met
uv run pytest -q --cov-branch --cov-report=term

(addopts already sets --cov=shenbi from Plan 1; adding --cov=src/shenbi here would double-specify and mismatch the coverage source.) 

Expected: line ≥ 90%, branch ≥ 80%.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run full test suite**

```bash
uv run pytest -q

Expected: ≥ 600 tests pass.

  • [ ] Step 4: Push + open PRs

7 PRs total (PR-28 ~ PR-34). Each can be independent PR.

docs.title: Spec coverage
docs.text: Spec PR Tasks PR-28 scoring tests Task 1 PR-29 phase_runner tests Task 2 PR-30 update_progress tests Task 3 PR-31 summarize_round tests Task 4 PR-32 gates direct-import tests Task 5 PR-33 hypothesis persistence Task 6 PR-34 pytest config Task 7

docs.title: Estimated test counts
docs.text:
Module Tests scoring ~60 + 10 property = 70 phase_runner ~50 + 8 property = 58 update_progress ~50 summarize_round ~40 gates/shared ~15 gates/g0-g7 ~80 (10 per gate × 8 gates) gates/g4/generic ~15 gates/g4/<20 skills> ~240 (12 per skill) Total NEW 568 Plus existing 43 Grand total 611

Arithmetic (20 per-skill G4 checkers, verified via grep -c "^def g4_"): 70 + 58 + 50 + 40 + 15 + 80 + 15 + 240 = 568 new, + 43 existing = 611. Density: 611 / 5921 framework LOC = 0.103 >= 0.10; meets the >= 600 test target. At 8 tests/skill the total is only ~531 (density 0.090 < 0.10), so the floor is 12 tests/skill. Do not lower the density floor to 0.08 -- that violates the "no cost compromise" directive from the spec README (the prior "30% then ramp" framing was removed for the same reason).

Property-test gap (spec Target State vs this plan): the spec Target State requires >= 60 property-based tests (>= 1 per pure function). This plan lands property tests for two modules only -- scoring (~10) and phase_runner (~8) = ~18 -- and adds none for update_progress, summarize_round, or the gates' pure functions (word_count_md, count_transition_words, _normalize_file_paths, jload, yload). ~18 is ~30% of the spec target. This is an acknowledged, deliberate initial landing: the priority is reaching the density floor and coverage with direct-import unit tests. The remaining ~42 property tests are a follow-up: add tests/unit/*_property.py for update_progress, summarize_round, and shenbi.gates.shared (>= 1 per pure function) to reach >= 60. Track this as a gap -- do not mark the spec Target State "property-based tests >= 60" row as met until the follow-up lands.

Density denominator vs Plan 3 PR-27: see Task 8 Step 1. If PR-27 has landed, the denominator is ~6489 (not 5921) and 611/6489 = 0.094 < 0.10; add tests (or count PR-27 Step 6's skill_utils tests) until the live $loc clears 0.10.

docs.title: Risk highlights
docs.text:
• Test-writing is repetitive. Use code generation or agent subagents per module.
  • Property tests may find bugs. Fix bugs as found, commit counterexamples.
  • PR-32 is the biggest PR (~400 tests). Split per-skill if reviewer complains.

docs.title: Execution Handoff
docs.text:
Plan saved. Two execution options:

1. Subagent-Driven (recommended) — one subagent per module's tests. 2. Inline Execution — batch in this session.

After this plan completes, Plan 3 PR-26 (density test) passes and PR-26 can merge.

docs.title: P-1.E Plan 5: CI / Supply Chain Hardening (PR-35 ~ PR-39)
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development or superpowers:executing-plans.

Goal: Harden CI: add uv lock --check, enable cache, add Python 3.13, add CodeQL, fix renovate policy, automate pre-commit autoupdate, create release workflow with SBOM attach.

Architecture: Five independent PRs touching only .github/workflows/ + renovate.json + .pre-commit-config.yaml. No source code changes.

Tech Stack: GitHub Actions, uv 0.10+, CodeQL, Renovate, softprops/action-gh-release.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-p-1.e-foundation-completion/05-ci-supply-chain.md

Depends on: Plan 1 PR-18 (src layout) + Plan 2 PR-23 (mutation comparator + baseline) + Plan 3 PR-25 (remove mypy overrides).

Blocks: None (CI work doesn't block framework functionality).

Estimated effort: M (3-5 days).

docs.title: Task 0: Pre-flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Verify Plan 1 merged: git log --oneline | grep "PR-18"
  • [ ] Verify Plan 3 PR-25 merged: git log --oneline | grep "PR-25"
  • [ ] Verify src layout exists: test -d src/shenbi (halt if missing).
  • [ ] Verify mutation-testing deps exist (Plan 2 PR-23): test -f tools/compare_mutation_score.py and test -f tests/baselines/mutation-score.txt. Halt if missing; the mutation job is non-functional without them.
  • [ ] Verify [tool.basedpyright] include covers src/shenbi/ (PR-25 scope). Halt if missing; basedpyright silently skips the source tree otherwise and CI reports green on an empty analysis.
  • [ ] Verify mutmut CLI surface: uv run mutmut run --help. The mutation job below assumes mutmut 3.x has no --paths-to-mutate or --use-cache flags (contradicting the spec PR-35 YAML). If those flags DO exist, use them for incremental mutation testing instead of the unscoped mutmut run.
  • [ ] Verify clean tree.
  • [ ] Verify CI passes: push a trivial no-op change (e.g., a README whitespace tweak), CI runs green. GitHub rejects empty pushes.

docs.title: PR-35: CI Hardening (uv lock check + cache + Python 3.13 + mutation)
Task 1: Update .github/workflows/ci.yml
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: .github/workflows/ci.yml

  • [ ] Step 1: Add uv lock --check + cache + Python 3.13 + mutation job

Preserve the name:, on:, and concurrency: blocks above jobs: (the current ci.yml has name: CI, push/PR triggers, and a concurrency group with cancel-in-progress: true). Replace only from jobs: to EOF with:

jobs:
  quality:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13"]
    # 3.13 allow-failure during rollout (spec Risk table).
    # Windows allow-failure until @pytest.mark.skip_on_windows marker is
    # registered (spec OQ-1; deferred to a follow-up source-code PR).
    continue-on-error: ${{ matrix.python-version == '3.13' || matrix.os == 'windows-latest' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v3
        with:
          enable-cache: true
          cache-dependency-glob: "uv.lock"
      - run: uv python install ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      - name: Verify uv.lock is up to date
        run: uv lock --check
      - run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev
      - run: uv run ruff check .
      - run: uv run ruff format --check .
      - run: uv run mypy src/shenbi/
      - run: uv run basedpyright
      - name: Run tests (parallel, excluding last)
        run: uv run pytest -n auto -m "not last" --hypothesis-profile=ci
      - name: Run coverage threshold test (serial, last only)
        run: uv run pytest -p no:xdist -m "last" --no-cov --hypothesis-profile=ci

  dependency-review:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Dependency review (license + vulnerability)
        uses: actions/dependency-review-action@v4

  action-validation:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v3
        with:
          enable-cache: true
      - run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev
      - name: Validate GitHub Actions YAML
        run: uv run yamllint --strict .github/workflows/

  mutation-testing:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v3
        with:
          enable-cache: true
      - run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev
      - name: Run mutation testing
        run: |
          # mutmut 3.x reads source_paths from [tool.mutmut] config;
          # per-file CLI scoping is not supported (no --paths-to-mutate
          # or --use-cache flags in 3.x). Changed-file detection only
          # decides whether to run mutmut at all.
          BASE="origin/${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}"
          CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only "${BASE}...HEAD" \
            -- 'src/shenbi/')
          if [ -z "$CHANGED" ]; then
            echo "No Python changes; skipping mutation testing"
            exit 0
          fi
          uv run mutmut run
      - name: Compare to baseline (blocks on regression > 5%)
        run: |
          # compare_mutation_score.py runs 'mutmut results' internally
          # and parses output; it only accepts --baseline and --threshold
          # (Plan 2 PR-23). No --current flag exists.
          uv run python tools/compare_mutation_score.py \
            --baseline tests/baselines/mutation-score.txt
  • [ ] Step 2: Verify YAML syntax
uv run yamllint --strict .github/workflows/ci.yml

Expected: no errors.

  • [ ] Step 3: Commit + push
git add .github/workflows/ci.yml
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-35): harden CI

- Add 'uv lock --check' to detect stale lockfile
- Enable uv cache (setup-uv enable-cache: true)
- Add Python 3.13 to matrix
- Add mutation-testing job (mutmut 3.x), blocks on regression > 5%
- Add dependency-review job (license + vulnerability scan on PRs)
"
git push
  • [ ] Step 4: Open PR + verify CI green across the 4 hard-fail combinations (2 non-Windows OS x 2 stable Pythons = 4). The 3 Python-3.13 entries and the 3 Windows entries use allow-failure and may be amber.

docs.title: Task 2: Create .github/workflows/codeql.yml
docs.text:
Files: - Create: .github/workflows/codeql.yml

  • [ ] Step 1: Write CodeQL workflow
name: CodeQL
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 4 * * 1'  # Weekly Monday 04:00 UTC

jobs:
  analyze:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      actions: read
      contents: read
      security-events: write
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        language: [python]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
        with:
          languages: ${{ matrix.language }}
          queries: +security-and-quality
      - uses: github/codeql-action/autobuild@v3
      - uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3
  • [ ] Step 2: Commit
git add .github/workflows/codeql.yml
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-36): add CodeQL static security analysis

Runs on every PR + weekly. Python language only (no JS/TS in scope).
queries: +security-and-quality enables extended query suite."
git push
  • [ ] Step 3: After PR merges, verify CodeQL appears in GitHub Security tab.

docs.title: PR-37: Renovate Policy Fix (minor → human review)
Task 3: Update renovate.json
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: renovate.json

  • [ ] Step 1: Replace renovate.json

Note: This uses matchDepPatterns (the current preferred Renovate field) instead of the spec's matchPackagePatterns. Both are valid; matchDepPatterns is the newer alias.

{
  "$schema": "https://docs.renovatebot.com/renovate-schema.json",
  "extends": ["config:recommended", ":semanticCommits"],
  "schedule": ["before 6am on monday"],
  "timezone": "Asia/Shanghai",
  "packageRules": [
    {
      "groupName": "ruff",
      "matchDepPatterns": ["ruff"]
    },
    {
      "groupName": "pytest",
      "matchDepPatterns": ["pytest"]
    },
    {
      "description": "Patch updates can automerge after CI passes",
      "matchUpdateTypes": ["patch"],
      "automerge": true,
      "automergeType": "pr",
      "automergeStrategy": "squash"
    },
    {
      "description": "Minor updates require human review (may include behavioral changes)",
      "matchUpdateTypes": ["minor"],
      "automerge": false,
      "labels": ["minor-bump"]
    },
    {
      "description": "Major updates require human review and labels",
      "matchUpdateTypes": ["major"],
      "automerge": false,
      "labels": ["major-bump"]
    },
    {
      "description": "Pre-commit hook revs updated weekly",
      "matchManagers": ["pre-commit"],
      "automerge": false,
      "labels": ["pre-commit"]
    }
  ],
  "vulnerabilityAlerts": {
    "enabled": true,
    "labels": ["security"],
    "automerge": true,
    "automergeType": "pr",
    "automergeStrategy": "squash"
  }
}
  • [ ] Step 2: Validate JSON
python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('renovate.json'))"

Expected: no error.

  • [ ] Step 3: Commit
git add renovate.json
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-37): narrow automerge to patch-only updates

Minor/major updates now require human review. Pre-commit hook revs
get explicit 'pre-commit' label. Vulnerability alerts still automerge
after CI passes (security urgency)."

docs.title: Task 4: Remove landmine + add autoupdate workflow
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: .pre-commit-config.yaml (remove registry-lockfile-fresh hook) - Create: .github/workflows/pre-commit-autoupdate.yml

  • [ ] Step 1: Remove the landmine hook

In .pre-commit-config.yaml, find and DELETE this block:

      - id: registry-lockfile-fresh
        name: registry.lock.json freshness (P0 scaffolding, no-op in P-1)
        entry: tests/build_registry.py
        language: system
        files: ^(skills/.*\.yaml|tests/tiers/.*\.yaml)$
        pass_filenames: false

The hook will be re-added in P0 when tests/build_registry.py (or src/shenbi/registry/build.py) actually exists.

  • [ ] Step 2: Verify pre-commit still runs cleanly
uv run pre-commit run --all-files

Expected: all hooks pass.

  • [ ] Step 3: Create autoupdate workflow

.github/workflows/pre-commit-autoupdate.yml:

name: Pre-commit autoupdate
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 2 * * 1'  # Monday 02:00 UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  autoupdate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.13"
      - run: pip install pre-commit
      - run: pre-commit autoupdate
      - name: Create PR if changes
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v6
        with:
          commit-message: "chore: pre-commit autoupdate"
          title: "chore: pre-commit autoupdate"
          branch: pre-commit-autoupdate
          labels: dependencies, pre-commit
  • [ ] Step 4: Commit
git add .pre-commit-config.yaml .github/workflows/pre-commit-autoupdate.yml
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-38): remove build_registry.py landmine + add autoupdate workflow

The registry-lockfile-fresh hook referenced tests/build_registry.py which
doesn't exist. It was a no-op today (files: matched nothing) but would
fail the moment P0 creates skills/<name>/meta.yaml. Hook will be re-added
in P0 when the build tool actually exists.

Added weekly workflow to run 'pre-commit autoupdate' and create PR if
any hook revs are outdated."

docs.title: Task 5: Create .github/workflows/release.yml
docs.text:
Files: - Create: .github/workflows/release.yml

  • [ ] Step 1: Write release workflow
name: Release
on:
  push:
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v3
        with:
          enable-cache: true
      - run: uv sync --frozen --extra dev
      - name: Generate SBOM (CycloneDX)
        run: uv run cyclonedx-py environment -o sbom.cdx.json
      - name: Build sdist and wheel
        run: uv build
      - name: Generate changelog for tag
        id: changelog
        run: |
          prev_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 HEAD^ 2>/dev/null || echo "")
          if [ -n "$prev_tag" ]; then
            notes=$(git log --format='- %s (%h)' ${prev_tag}..HEAD)
          else
            # First release: cap to recent 100 commits, not full history
            notes=$(git log --format='- %s (%h)' -n 100)
          fi
          echo "notes<<EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "$notes" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "EOF" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          body: ${{ steps.changelog.outputs.notes }}
          files: |
            dist/*.whl
            dist/*.tar.gz
            sbom.cdx.json
  • [ ] Step 2: Verify YAML
uv run yamllint --strict .github/workflows/release.yml
  • [ ] Step 3: Test by pushing a test tag (optional)
git tag v0.2.0-rc1
git push origin v0.2.0-rc1

Verify GitHub Release created with wheel + sdist + SBOM attached. Delete tag after test:

git tag -d v0.2.0-rc1
git push origin :refs/tags/v0.2.0-rc1
# Also delete the GitHub Release via UI or `gh release delete v0.2.0-rc1`
  • [ ] Step 4: Commit
git add .github/workflows/release.yml
git commit -m "feat(P-1.E PR-39): add release workflow with SBOM attach

Triggers on tag push (v*). Builds wheel + sdist, generates CycloneDX SBOM,
creates GitHub Release with auto-generated changelog. All 3 artifacts
attached to release."

docs.title: Task 6: Final verification + open PRs
docs.text:
• [ ] Step 1: All 5 PRs (PR-35 ~ PR-39) merged.
  • [ ] Step 2: Trigger CodeQL manually (Actions tab → CodeQL → Run workflow). Verify completes.
  • [ ] Step 3: Verify pre-commit autoupdate workflow appears in Actions tab (will run next Monday 02:00 UTC).
  • [ ] Step 4: Push a test tag v0.2.0-rc1 to verify release workflow. Delete tag + release after.

docs.title: Spec coverage
docs.text: Spec PR Tasks PR-35 CI hardening Task 1 PR-36 CodeQL Task 2 PR-37 renovate policy Task 3 PR-38 pre-commit cleanup + autoupdate Task 4 PR-39 release workflow Task 5

docs.title: Cost-compromise check
docs.text:
• ✅ No "optional" or "best-effort" language
  • ✅ Mutation testing blocks on regression (per spec R6.6 fix)
  • ✅ CodeQL covers Python (single language in scope)
  • ✅ Release workflow attaches SBOM (SLSA Level 2-aligned; provenance generation deferred)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• PR-35 mutation-testing job requires tools/compare_mutation_score.py and tests/baselines/mutation-score.txt (both created in Plan 2 PR-23). Task 0 checks halt if missing.
  • PR-35 also depends on PR-25 updating [tool.basedpyright] include to cover src/shenbi/; otherwise basedpyright silently skips the new source tree.
  • PR-35 Windows entries match spec OQ-1 (Yes). They use continue-on-error until the @pytest.mark.skip_on_windows marker is registered (requires a conftest.py change, deferred to a follow-up source-code PR). Promote Windows to hard-fail once the marker lands.
  • PR-35 3.13 entries use continue-on-error per spec Risk table (allow-failure during rollout; promote once stable).
  • PR-38 is independent (can land anytime).

docs.title: Execution Handoff
docs.text:
Plan saved. Two execution options:

1. Subagent-Driven — one subagent per workflow file. 2. Inline Execution — all 5 PRs are small, can batch.

After this plan, all CI/CD hardening complete. Proceed to Plan 6 (Cluster 06 enterprise) and Plan 7 (Cluster 07 docs).

docs.title: P-1.E Plan 6: Enterprise Baseline Files (PR-40 ~ PR-43)
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development or superpowers:executing-plans.

Goal: Add standard enterprise files (LICENSE, SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODEOWNERS, CHANGELOG.md, PR/Issue templates), adopt PEP 735 dependency groups, add justfile task runner, expand mkdocs nav.

Architecture: Four independent PRs. PR-40 creates 12+ baseline files. PR-41 migrates [project.optional-dependencies] → [dependency-groups] + adds git-cliff. PR-42 adds justfile. PR-43 expands mkdocs.yml.

Tech Stack: PEP 735, just, git-cliff, mkdocs-material.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-p-1.e-foundation-completion/06-enterprise-and-polish.md

Depends on: Plan 1 (src layout).

Blocks: Plan 7 (docs).

Estimated effort: M (3-5 days).

docs.title: Task 0: Pre-flight
docs.text:
• [ ] Plan 1 merged. The justfile (uv run shenbi-validate, uv run shenbi-dispatch, uv run mypy src/shenbi/) and the mkdocstrings directives (::: shenbi.exceptions, ::: shenbi.logging) all require Plan 1's src layout and entry points. Verify before proceeding: 
    test -f src/shenbi/__init__.py \
  && grep -q '\[project.scripts\]' pyproject.toml \
  || { echo 'Plan 1 (src layout) not merged - aborting'; exit 1; }
  • [ ] Plan 2 awareness: the mutate-check justfile recipe (PR-42) calls tools/compare_mutation_score.py and tests/baselines/mutation-score.txt, both created by Plan 2 (PR-fraud). This plan does NOT hard-depend on Plan 2 -- the recipe has an inline guard comment and will error clearly if those files are absent. No merge-order constraint with Plan 2.
  • [ ] Clean tree.
